ip_outcome_note: Visual match confirmed: round blue tadpole with spiral-patterned belly matches National Pokedex no. 60 exactly. PokemonTCG.io API xy12-23 confirms name, types, and Ken Sugimori artist credit.

trivia_bullets.text: Poliwag (no. 23/108) is part of Evolutions (XY12, November 2016), the 20th-anniversary Base Set tribute. Art credited to Ken Sugimori, Game Freak's lead character designer and creator of the original Pokemon designs. Sugimori personally designed the Poliwag line.

Poliwag's belly spiral is modeled after the intestinal tract of real-life tadpoles, whose internal organs are visible through semitransparent abdominal skin. The Pokedex confirms: 'A part of its internal organs can be seen through the skin as a spiral pattern.' The spiral is said to look clearer after eating. Documented by Pokemon researcher Dr. Lava as one of the franchise's most biologically accurate design choices.
"Its slick black skin is thin and damp. A part of its internal organs can be seen through the skin as a spiral pattern."

Poliwag (National Pokedex no. 60) branches at its final evolution: Poliwrath via Water Stone vs. Politoed via King's Rock trade (introduced Gen II). This made Poliwag one of the franchise's first examples of a held-item-dependent branching final-evolution line.

related_cards.name: Poliwrath
related_cards.note: Water/Fighting final evolution via Water Stone

related_cards.name: Politoed
related_cards.note: Alternate final evolution via King's Rock trade; branching line
